raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.3,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"The draft contains two internal contradictions that are load-bearing if logical review is in scope: (1) the abstract claims 'a single seq2seq backbone that performs all three steps in one forward pass,' but §2 describes separate DPR, classifier, rewriter, and FiD reader components 'trained sequentially, each with its own objective' — a pipeline, not one forward pass; (2) §4 attributes gains to a 'supervision-free approach' where 'our model never sees gold AmbigNQ disambiguations during training,' but §3 states 'the rewriter is supervised on AmbigNQ disambiguated questions.' These contradictions directly undermine the contribution claim.","R1.6":"'Improve this draft' centrally implicates writing quality: sentence clarity, paragraph cohesion, and precision of phrasing are core deliverables of any draft-improvement delegation regardless of scope."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should I focus primarily on prose clarity and writing quality, or do you also want me to identify and resolve logical contradictions and unsupported claims — for example, the draft simultaneously asserts a single-pass end-to-end architecture and describes a sequential multi-component pipeline, and claims supervision-free training while reporting supervised fine-tuning on AmbigNQ disambiguations?"}

rationale.R1.2: The draft contains two internal contradictions that are load-bearing if logical review is in scope: (1) the abstract claims 'a single seq2seq backbone that performs all three steps in one forward pass,' but §2 describes separate DPR, classifier, rewriter, and FiD reader components 'trained sequentially, each with its own objective' — a pipeline, not one forward pass; (2) §4 attributes gains to a 'supervision-free approach' where 'our model never sees gold AmbigNQ disambiguations during training,' but §3 states 'the rewriter is supervised on AmbigNQ disambiguated questions.' These contradictions directly undermine the contribution claim.
rationale.R1.6: 'Improve this draft' centrally implicates writing quality: sentence clarity, paragraph cohesion, and precision of phrasing are core deliverables of any draft-improvement delegation regardless of scope.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus primarily on prose clarity and writing quality, or do you also want me to identify and resolve logical contradictions and unsupported claims — for example, the draft simultaneously asserts a single-pass end-to-end architecture and describes a sequential multi-component pipeline, and claims supervision-free training while reporting supervised fine-tuning on AmbigNQ disambiguations?
